updates.title: Claude Fable 5 Now Included in Max/Team Premium; Credits-Only for Pro Starting July 20
updates.summary: Starting July 20, 2026, Claude Fable 5 is permanently included in all Max and Team Premium plans at 50% of normal usage limits. Pro and Team Standard users retain access via usage credits at $10/million input tokens and $50/million output tokens, and receive a one-time $100 credit bonus. The promotional free-access period that had been repeatedly extended finally ended July 19, 2026 at 11:59 PM PT.
updates.accuracy_notes: Confirmed via official Claude X/Twitter post and multiple reputable tech outlets (TechTimes, Tech2, AndroidAuthority, UsageBox). Pricing figures ($10/$50 per million tokens) and end date (July 19, 11:59 PM PT) are verifiable. High confidence.

updates.title: I stopped approving Claude Code's commands after discovering its built-in sandbox
updates.summary: XDA Developers explores Claude Code's built-in sandbox mode — enabled via /sandbox with auto-allow — which uses OS-level restrictions (macOS Seatbelt on Mac, Bubblewrap on Linux/WSL2) to confine Claude to the project directory and restrict outbound network connections to pre-approved domains. The article argues that the sandbox makes blanket command approvals unnecessary and actually safer to skip, enabling more autonomous Claude Code operation without sacrificing security. Network access is restricted by default, and Claude Code requests approval before connecting to any new domain.
updates.accuracy_notes: Based on verifiable Claude Code feature documentation. macOS Seatbelt and Linux Bubblewrap are real OS-level sandboxing technologies used by Claude Code. The /sandbox command and auto-allow mode are confirmed features. No red flags — factual and practical.

updates.title: Claude Code 2.1.214 and 2.1.215 Released — Security Fixes and Workflow Control Changes
updates.summary: Claude Code 2.1.214 (July 18) patched critical permission-check bypasses affecting Windows PowerShell 5.1 sessions and Bash, fixed single-segment dir/** allow rules, and improved background job handling, login error messages, and session management. Claude Code 2.1.215 (July 19) changed the /verify and /code-review skills so they only activate when directly invoked by the user — preventing them from running automatically mid-session — giving developers more deliberate control over quality checks.
updates.accuracy_notes: Confirmed via official GitHub releases page (github.com/anthropics/claude-code/releases) and changelog tracking services. Version numbers, dates, and specific fixes are verifiable. High confidence on technical accuracy.

updates.title: Anthropic Raises Claude Code Weekly Limits 50% Through August 19, 2026
updates.summary: On July 18, 2026, Anthropic extended the Claude Code weekly limits promotion — originally set to end July 19 — through August 19, 2026, maintaining 50% higher weekly usage limits for Pro, Max, Team, and eligible seat-based Enterprise users. The extension was driven by competitive pressure from OpenAI's GPT-5.6 family and Kimi pricing, as well as user feedback on hitting caps. Free plan users and consumption-based Enterprise seats are not eligible for the promotion.
updates.accuracy_notes: Confirmed via Princeton AI Newsletter's July 18 memo, Progressive Robot, APIdog, and other outlets citing the official Anthropic announcement. The August 19 end date and eligible plans (Pro, Max, Team, seat-based Enterprise) are consistently reported. High confidence.
